audit_status.source_base: Greek Acts text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Tertullus accusation, Paul defense, Way, resurrection hope, Felix delay, Drusilla, righteousness/self-control/judgment, bribe expectation, two-year custody.

verses.restored: After five days the high priest Ananias came down with some elders and a certain advocate, Tertullus, who informed the governor against Paul.
When he was called, Tertullus began to accuse, saying, Since through you we obtain much peace, and reforms happen for this nation through your forethought,
we welcome it in every way and everywhere, most excellent Felix, with all thankfulness.
But so that I may not weary you further, I ask you to hear us briefly in your fairness.
For having found this man a pest, stirring up disputes among all the Judeans throughout the inhabited world, and a ringleader of the sect of the Nazarenes,
who also attempted to profane the temple, we seized him.
[Some later manuscripts add material about Lysias taking him away by force and ordering accusers to come.]
By examining him yourself, you will be able to know concerning all these things of which we accuse him.
The Judeans also joined in the attack, affirming these things to be so.
Paul answered, when the governor motioned to him to speak: Knowing that you have been judge over this nation for many years, I cheerfully make my defense concerning myself.
You are able to know that it is not more than twelve days since I went up to worship in Jerusalem.
They did not find me in the temple disputing with anyone or making a crowd gather, neither in the synagogues nor throughout the city.
Nor are they able to prove to you the things of which they now accuse me.
But this I confess to you: according to the Way that they call a sect, thus I serve the Divine of our fathers, trusting all things according to the Law and written in the Prophets,
having hope in the Divine, which these also themselves await, that there will be a resurrection of both righteous/just and unjust.
In this also I myself practice having an untroubled conscience toward the Divine and humans through all.
After many years I arrived bringing acts of mercy to my nation and offerings.
In these they found me purified in the temple, not with crowd or disturbance. But there were some Judeans from Asia,
who ought to be present before you and accuse if they had anything against me.
Or let these themselves say what wrong they found when I stood before the council,
except concerning this one voice that I cried while standing among them: Concerning resurrection of the dead I am judged before you today.
Felix, knowing more accurately the things concerning the Way, postponed them, saying, When Lysias the commander comes down, I will decide your case.
He ordered the centurion to keep him, yet to have relief, and to prevent none of his own from serving him.
After some days, Felix came with Drusilla his wife, who was Judean, and sent for Paul and heard him concerning trust in Messiah Jesus.
As he reasoned concerning righteousness/justice, self-control, and the coming judgment, Felix became afraid and answered, For now go; when I find time, I will summon you.
At the same time he also hoped that silver would be given to him by Paul; therefore he sent for him more frequently and conversed with him.
When two years were fulfilled, Felix received Porcius Festus as successor; and wishing to lay up favor for the Judeans, Felix left Paul bound.
